title: Branch-less out-of-gas revert using returndatacopy for range validation
trigger_patterns: revert if value exceeds bit width
explanation: returndatacopy(dest, src, len) with len = sub(0, shr(48, number())): if block.number fits in 48 bits, shr(48, number()) = 0, sub(0, 0) = 0, len = 0, no-op. If block.number >= 2^48, shr(48, number()) >= 1, sub(0, 1) = type(uint256).max (wraps), returndatacopy tries to copy max uint256 bytes from empty returndata, causing an out-of-gas revert. No branch opcode needed — the copy itself is the conditional revert.
when_to_apply: Any uint48/uint32/uint16 range check on block.number or timestamp where an if-revert would otherwise be needed
when_not_to_apply: When the OOG behaviour needs to be distinguishable from other reverts, in view functions where gas is irrelevant
